key_insight: Materials discovery is most effective when theory, computation, experiment, machine learning, and quantum computing are integrated into reproducible workflows that reflect real-world complexity.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, more reliable materials selection and decision-making for deployable engineering applications by improving prediction quality and confidence under realistic conditions.
method: Use classical simulations to explore broad design spaces and screen candidates.
Combine experiments with computation to capture degradation, heterogeneity, and kinetic effects.
Apply machine learning and quantum methods where they are supported by curated data and property-relevant targets.
evidence_signal: The abstract emphasizes that reliable progress depends on well-curated datasets, clear provenance, uncertainty quantification, and realistic benchmarks rather than any single method alone.
watch_item: Machine learning and quantum approaches can be misleading or hard to operationalize if data quality, provenance, and workflow reproducibility are not carefully managed.
paper_title: The Future of Computing for Materials Science Challenges
